fields.helpText: This is a number field to allow adding new entries by number.
This is Level I of NUCC's structure of the Provider Taxonomy.

This is Level I, Provider Type - 2 byte alphanumeric consisting of the 1st and 2nd characters of X12 CODE:   A code that identifies a major grouping of service(s) or occupation(s) of health care providers.
This is the CMS (X12) Classification (Level II).

This is Level II, Classification Code - 2 byte alphanumeric consisting of the 3rd and 4th characters of the X12 CODE:   A code that identifies more specific services(s) or occupation(s) within the health care provider type. The coding is based on licensed provider classifications.
This is Level III in NUCC's structure of the Provider Taxonomy.  It is the most specific, but sometimes defines a "sub-category" of Classification.

This is Level III, Area of Specialization - a 5 byte alphanumeric consisting of the 5th through 9th characters of the X12 CODE:   A code that identifies the provider's specialization, a segment of the population that a health care provider chooses to service, a specific medical service, a specialization in treating a specific disease, or any other descriptive characteristic about the providers practice relating to the services rendered.
This field will allow old entries to be disabled without removing them from the table.
This field holds the date that this Class was inactivated.
This field holds the 7 character VA assigned code for national roll-up.
This is the code assigned by X12N.  X12 published the joint X12N and CMS Health Care Provider Taxonomy following the June 1997 X12 meeting.
This field is only to help a conversion routine for updates to the file. Any data is used only be a update routine.
This field holds the specialty code associated with the classification

This field is populated when one Term/Concept replaces another  Term/Concept.  This field is controlled by standardization and should  only be changed by standardization processes.  This field contains a  pointer to a VHA standard term that replaces this entry.
This field identifies the Master entry for a VUID associated with a  Term/Concept.
VHA Unique ID (VUID). A unique meaningless integer assigned to reference terms VHA wide.
Describes the pair Status and Effective Date/Time for each reference term.

This field indicates whether the entry is for an Individual or for a Non-Individual, and was added at the request of IHS in their namespace, until the file supporting Non-Individuals can be defined.   Note that the current definition of the X12 CODE does not explicitly indicate whether an entry is for an Individual or for a Non-Individual, either in value or structure definition.
